name: AreYouThereRequest
description: Allows any CFX endpoint to determine if another particular CFX endpoint is present on a CFX network. The response sends basic information about the endpoint, including its CFX Handle, and network hostname / address.
properties: AreYouThereRequest:  | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint to which the request is directed

name: AreYouThereResponse
description: Allows any CFX endpoint to determine if another particular CFX endpoint is present on a CFX network. The response sends basic information about the endpoint, including its CFX Handle, and network hostname / address.
properties: AreYouThereResponse: Default constructor | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint that is responding | RequestNetworkUri: The network address / Uri to be used for requests to this endpoint | RequestTargetAddress: The AMQP 1.0 target address to be used for requests to this endpoint | Result: The result of the request

name: EndpointConnected
description: Sent when an Endpoint joins a CFX network after it has been established. Provides the same information as the response to the AreYouThereRequest message.
properties: EndpointConnected:  | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint that is responding | RequestNetworkUri: The network address / Uri to be used for requests to this endpoint | RequestTargetAddress: The AMQP 1.0 target address to be used for requests to this endpoint

name: EndpointShuttingDown
description: Sent when an endpoint is about to shut down and disconnect from a CFX network
properties: EndpointShuttingDown:  | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint that is shutting down

name: GetEndpointInformationRequest
description: Requests detailed information about a single endpoint, as specified by its CFX Handle.
properties: GetEndpointInformationRequest:  | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint about which the sender wishes to obtain information.

name: GetEndpointInformationResponse
description: Allows any CFX endpoint to request the capabilities of a specified single endpoint. The response includes information about the endpoint, including its CFX Handle, and network hostname / address.
properties: GetEndpointInformationResponse: Default constructor | EndpointInformation: Dynamic structure that describes the details of this endpoint. | Result: The result of the request

name: Heartbeat
description: All endpoints are obligated to publish a Heartbeast message on all Publish type no less than once every 5 minutes. The class automatically performs this function for you when using the CFX SDK.
properties: Heartbeat:  | ActiveFaults: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.7 ** Any faults currently active on this endpoint. Faults should be repeated here until cleared. Leaving this parameter as null means that active faults are not known. Setting it to an empty List implies positive knowledge that there are no active faults. | ActiveRecipes: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.7 ** Any recipes currently active on this endpoint. Recipes may be specified either using their identifiers or by including recipe details such as expected cycle times depending on what information the endpoing is able to communicate. Leaving this parameter as null means that active recipes are not known. Setting it to an empty List implies positive knowledge that there are no active recipes. Multiple recipes may be active at once on a given endpoint. | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint that is publishing the Heartbeat. | HeartbeatFrequency: The amount of time to expect between Heartbeast messasges for this endpoint

name: NotSupportedResponse
description: Allows a CFX endpoint to indicate to the sender of a request that it is not able to answer to this particular request There can be several reasons : - Unknown request/response message : appeared in a more recent version of CFX than the one used by the endp
properties: NotSupportedResponse: Default constructor | RequestResult: The result of the request

name: WhoIsThereRequest
description: Allows any CFX endpoint to discover all of the other endpoints participating in this CFX network, and their capabilities. All other CFX endpoints matching the specified criteria must then respond to this broadcast.
properties: WhoIsThereRequest: Default Constructor | SupportedTopicQueryType: If the sender is interested only in certain types of endpoints, tbis property specifies how SupportedTopics list will be interpreted. | SupportedTopics: An optional list of CFX topics. Only machines supporting any or all of these CFX topics will respond to this broadcast.

name: WhoIsThereResponse
description: Allows any CFX endpoint to discover all of the other endpoints participating in this CFX network, and their capabilities. All other CFX endpoints must then respond to this broadcast, providing information about themselves.
properties: WhoIsThereResponse: Default Constructor | CFXHandle: The handle of the endpoint that is responding | RequestNetworkUri: The network address / Uri to be used for requests to this endpoint | RequestTargetAddress: The AMQP 1.0 target address to be used for requests to this endpoint | Result: Result of the request.

name: FileTransferRequest
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Sent by an endpoint to another endpoint to initiate a file transfer. File transfers may proceed either from the initiator/requester to the recipient (PUSH Mode), OR from recipient to initiator/requester (PULL Mode).
properties: FileTransferRequest: Default constructor | File: For file transfers initiated at the source (FileTransferDirection.Push), contains information about the file to be transferred. For file transfers initiated by the destination (FileTransferDirection.Pull), this property should be NULL. For FileTransferMode.InBand, this structure will contain the file payload (data) itself. For FileTransferMode.OutOfBand, this structure will contain a secure link/URL that can be used by the destination to obtain the file payload data. | FileLocation: An optional property that may be used to indicate the location to the file on the source system. This need not be a physical path. It could be as virtual path, as in the case of a web or FTP server, or file share. Alternatively, it need not represent a traditional path at all. For example, it could be a lookup key to locate the file within a NoSQL database environment. The format of this property is dependent on the nature of the source system. | FileTitle: The title of the file, EXCLUDING any path information, but INCLUDING file extension when appropriate. | TransferDirection: Specifies the direction of transfer. File transfer may proceed from requestor to responder (Push), or from responder to requester (Pull). In Push mode, the file token is contained within the request itself. In Pull mode, the file token is contained within the response message. | TransferMode: Specifies the mode of transfer. File data may be transferred within the CFX messages themselves (In-Band), or transferred via a separate secure protocol (Out-of-Band), such as HTTPS, SFTP, etc. Direct, in-band file transfer is not recommended for larger data files. Many AMQP message broker platforms limit the maximum total message size of an AMQP message. Microsoft's Azure message bus, for example, limits messages to 256KB for a standard subscription, and 1MB for a premium subscription. RabbitMQ recommends limiting messages to 128MB or less. Use OutOfBand transfer for larger file sizes.

name: FileTransferResponse
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Sent by an endpoint in response to a . File transfers may proceed either from the initiator/requester to the recipient (PUSH Mode), OR from recipient to initiator/requester (PULL Mode).
properties: FileTransferResponse: Default constructor | File: For file transfers initiated at the destination (FileTransferDirection.Pull), contains information about the file to be transferred. For file transfers initiated by the source (FileTransferDirection.Push), this property should be NULL. For FileTransferMode.InBand, this structure will contain the file payload (data) itself. For FileTransferMode.OutOfBand, this structure will contain a secure link/URL that can be used by the destination to obtain the file payload data. | FileLocation: An optional property that may be used to indicate the location to the file on the source system. This need not be a physical path. It could be as virtual path, as in the case of a web or FTP server, or file share. Alternatively, it need not represent a traditional path at all. For example, it could be a lookup key to locate the file within a NoSQL database environment. The format of this property is dependent on the nature of the source system. | FileTitle: The title of the file, EXCLUDING any path information, but INCLUDING file extension when appropriate. | Result: Result of the request | TransferMode: Specifies the mode of transfer. File data may be transferred within the CFX messages themselves (In-Band), or transferred via a separate secure protocol (Out-of-Band), such as HTTPS, SFTP, etc. Direct, in-band file transfer is not recommended for larger data files. Many AMQP message broker platforms limit the maximum total message size of an AMQP message. Microsoft's Azure message bus, for example, limits messages to 256KB for a standard subscription, and 1MB for a premium subscription. RabbitMQ recommends limiting messages to 128MB or less. Use OutOfBand transfer for larger file sizes.

name: ValidateOperatorRequest
description: Request that an operator take action now or be responsible for a process endpoint. The opposite endpoint can accept or reject this. Can be used if the MES has advanced user management. Multiple operators must be requested separately.
properties: ValidateOperatorRequest:  | Operator: A structure which defines the Operator.

name: ValidateOperatorResponse
description: Response to a request that the operator's login was successful or not.
properties: ValidateOperatorResponse:  | Result: The result of the request

name: WorkOrdersScheduled
description: Sent when a Work Order (or Work Order sub-batch) has been scheduled to be executed at a particular work area at a particular time.
properties: WorkOrdersScheduled: Default constructor. | ScheduledWorkOrders: A list of Work Orders that have been scheduled.

name: WorkOrdersUnscheduled
description: Sent when a previously scheduled Work Order (or Work Order sub-batch) has been unscheduled at a particular work area at a particular time.
properties: WorkOrdersUnscheduled: Default constructor. | ScheduledWorkOrderIdentifiers: A list of Work Orders that have been scheduled.

name: ValidateTopicRequest
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.0 **Request that a specific topic needs to be validated before continuing the process / operations.
properties: ValidateTopicRequest:  | ValidationTopic: An enum for the specific validation topic of the request. | ValidationTopicData: A structure which defines the ValidationTopicData fo the request

name: ValidateTopicResponse
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.0 **Response to a request that a specific topic (e.g., tool, material carrier, recipe, material) was successfull validated or not.
properties: ValidateTopicResponse:  | Result: The result of the request | ValidationTopic: An enum for the specific validation topic of the request.

name: ValidateUnitsRequest
description: Sent from a process endpoint in order to validate the identifier of the next production unit. Process endpoints, where configured, should send this request before allowing the next unit to enter the process.
properties: ValidateUnitsRequest: Default constructor | HermesIdentifier: The Hermes BoardId of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. HermesIdentifier will be transfered between all machines which support Hermes. The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well. | Lane: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.2 ** Lane identifier. Null if no specific lane | PrimaryIdentifier: Identification used for the carrier (or the unit itself if no carrier) | Units: List of structures that identify each specific instance of production unit that arrived (could be within a carrier or panel). | Validations: List of validations to be performed”. Options are: UnitRouteValidation", "UnitStatusValidation"

name: ValidateUnitsResponse
description: Sent from a process endpoint in order to validate the identifier of the next production unit. Process endpoints, where configured, should send this request before allowing the next unit to enter the process.
properties: ValidateUnitsResponse: Default constructor | PrimaryResult: Overall result of the validation request | Result: Result of the request | ValidationResults: Individual results of the validation request (for multiple units in a carrier or PCB Panel)

name: MaterialsRemovedFromDryStorage
description: Sent when one or more MSD material packages have been removed from dry storage
properties: MaterialsRemovedFromDryStorage: Default constructor | Materials: The materials which have been removed from dry storage

name: MaterialsRestored
description: Sent when one or more MSD material packages have been reconditioned, and restored for use in production
properties: MaterialsRestored: Default constructor | Materials: The materials which have been restored

name: GetLoadedMaterialsRequest
description: A request to a material storage endpoint to obtain a list of all the materials currently stored within the endpoint.
properties: GetLoadedMaterialsRequest:  | LocationIdentifiers: An optional list of specific locations in which the requestor is interested. If empty, all materials loaded at the Endpoint are returned. | ResourceUniqueIdentifier: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.7 ** The unique identifier of the resource in which the requestor is interested (optional) If set to null, the search is made on the endpoint itself. The use of this property should be reserved to an endpoint that is able to know the location of all the MaterialPackage of the factory, like an ERP for example

name: GetLoadedMaterialsResponse
description: A response to a request to a material storage endpoint to obtain a list of all the materials currently stored within the endpoint.
properties: GetLoadedMaterialsResponse: Default constructor | Materials: A list of the materials stored at the process endpoint, including storage location information. | Result: The result of the request

name: MaterialCarriersLoaded
description: Sent when a material carrier (typcially containing 1 or more material packages) is loaded at an endpoint.
properties: MaterialCarriersLoaded: Default constructor | Carriers: There are two usage cases are available for this message: 1) Load MaterialCarrier directly to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = Carrier to be loaded) 2) Load MateriialCarrier to another MaterialCarrier (LocationIdentifier = Identifier of new parent carrier, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded)

name: MaterialCarriersUnloaded
description: Sent when a material carrier (typcially containing 1 or more material packages) is unloaded at an endpoint.
properties: MaterialCarriersUnloaded: Default constructor | CarrierIdentifiers: List of carriers to be unloaded. NOTE - This message DOES NOT mean that the MaterialPackage is unloaded from the MaterialCarrier. Only that the MaterialCarrier is unloaded from its parent (slot on and Endpoint, or another MaterialCarrier).

name: MaterialsEmpty
description: Sent when one or more material packages stored at a particular location become fully exhausted / depleted.
properties: MaterialsEmpty: Default constructor | EmptyMaterials: A list of the material locations where the depleted material packages are stored or loaded at the endpoint. Also includes the material package that is loaded at the location (if known). For example, if the endpoint is an SMT placement machine, the location identifies the feeder slot/position where the depleted material package is loaded.

name: MaterialsLoaded
description: Sent when a material package (potentially contained within a material carrier) is loaded at an endpoint.
properties: MaterialsLoaded: Default constructor | Materials: There are three usage cases are available for this message: 1) Load MaterialPackage directly to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = null) 2) Load MaterialPackage to MaterialCarrier (LocationIdentifier = null, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded) 3) Load MaterialPackage to MaterialCarrier AND Load MaterialCarrier to Endpoint (LocationIdentifier = Endpoint Slot Identifier, CarrierInformation = Identifier of carrier to be loaded)

name: MaterialsUnloaded
description: Sent when a material package (potentially contained within a material carrier) is unloaded at an endpoint.
properties: MaterialsUnloaded: Default constructor | MaterialPackageIdentifiers: List of material package identifiers to be unloaded.

name: SplicePointDetected
description: Sent when an endpoint detects a splice point. A splice point is a juncture between two different material packages of the same part that have been pre-joined prior to loading at the material location, or were joined-in-place during production.
properties: SplicePointDetected: Default Constructor | DepletedMaterial: Describes the location on the machine where a splice point was detected, along with information about the material package that has been depleted. | NewlyActiveMaterial: Describes the location on the machine where a splice point was detected, along with information about the new material package that is now being actively consumed at that location.

name: ValidateStationSetupRequest
description: Request to a process endpoint to validate that the currently loaded materials comply with the setup requirements supplied in this request.
properties: ValidateStationSetupRequest:  | SetupRequirements: The setup requirements to be validated

name: ValidateStationSetupResponse
description: Response to a request to a process endpoint to validate that the currently loaded materials comply with the setup requirements supplied by the request.
properties: ValidateStationSetupResponse: Default constructor | Result: The result of the request

name: CheckpointReached
description: Sent when a group of materials or production units that is being transported through the factory environment arrives at a way point along its path source to destination.
properties: CheckpointReached:  | Checkpoint: The name of the check point that was reached | Comments: Human readable comments related to this arrival (where applicable) | FinalDestination: The name of the final destination of this transport order | NextCheckpoint: The name of the next anticipated check point along the transport order's path from source to final destination | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order number (optional, where applicable) | Status: The status of this transport order at the time of the event | TrackedBy: The operator who recorded the arrival (optional, where applicable) | TransportOrderId: The number of the related transport order

name: CreateTransportOrderRequest
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.0 ** Message request to create transport order; it shall contain all necessary data for one or more transports while leaving sufficient flexibility to make use of different Fleet Manager capabilities.
properties: CreateTransportOrderRequest:  | FinalDestination: The final destination for this transport order | Materials: A list of the specific materials, WIP, and / or production units that are to be transported for this transport order | Priority: The priority low – medium – high for the new transport order | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order ID (where applicable) | Source: The point of origination for this this transport order | StartedBy: The application or operator who initiated the new transport order | TargetDeliveryTime: The target delivery time for completing the new transport order | TransportOrderId: The order ID of the new transport order

name: CreateTransportOrderResponse
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.0 ** Message response for a corresponding transport order request.
properties: CreateTransportOrderResponse:  | FleetManagerTransportOrderId: The order ID created by the Fleet Manager for this transport order | Result: The result of the request | TransportOrderId: The order ID of the queried transport order

name: GetTransportOrderStatusRequest
description: A request to an endpoint (such as an upper level system, MES, ERP, etc.) to check the status of a particular transport order. A transport order is a directive to move materials / WIP / production units from one location to another.
properties: GetTransportOrderStatusRequest:  | TransportOrderId: The order number of the related transport order

name: GetTransportOrderStatusResponse
description: A response to a request to an endpoint (such as an upper level system, MES, ERP, etc.) to check the status of a particular transport order.
properties: GetTransportOrderStatusResponse: Default constructor | FinalDestination: The final destination of this transport order | History: The history of this transport order | Result: The result of the request | Status: The current status of this transport order | TransportOrderId: The order number of the related transport order

name: StartTransferRequest
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.
properties: StartTransferRequest:  | Materials: A list of the specific materials, WIP, and / or production units that are to be transported for this transport order | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order ID (where applicable) | StartedBy: The application or operator who initiated the new transport order | TransportOrderId: The order ID of the new transport order

name: StartTransferResponse
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 2.
properties: StartTransferResponse:  | Result: The result of the request | TransportOrderId: The order ID of the queried transport order

name: TransportOrderCompleted
description: Sent when a transport order has arrived at its final destination.
properties: TransportOrderCompleted:  | AcceptedBy: The operator who accepted the delivery, and recorded the arrival (optional) | Comments: Human readable comments related to this arrival event (when applicable) | FinalDestination: The final destination where the transport order was delivered) | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order number (where applicable) | TransportOrderId: The order number of the related transport order

name: TransportOrderStarted
description: Sent when a new transport order is initiated. A transport order is a directive to move materials / WIP / production units from one location to another.
properties: TransportOrderStarted: Default constructor | Comments: Human readable comments related to this event | FinalDestination: The final destination for this transport order | Materials: A list of the specific materials, WIP, and / or production units that are to be transported for this transport order. | NextCheckpoint: The next anticipated check point (way point) for this transport order | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order number (where applicable) | Source: The point of origination for this this transport order. Often will be a stock area, room, etc., but may be any location within the factory environment | StartedBy: The operator who initiated the new transport order (optional, where applicable) | Status: The status of this transport order at the time of the event | TransportOrderId: The order number of the new transport order

  "Materials": []
}

name: TransportOrderStatusChanged
description: Sent when the status of an existing transport order has changed.
properties: TransportOrderStatusChanged: Default constructor | Comments: Human readable comments related to this event | FinalDestination: The final destination for this transport order | NextCheckpoint: The next anticipated check point (way point) for this transport order | RelatedWorkOrderId: The related production work order number (where applicable) | Source: The point of origination for this this transport order. Often will be a stock area, room, etc., but may be any location within the factory environment | Status: The status of this transport order at the time of the event | TransportOrderId: The order number of the new transport order | UpdatedBy: The operator who updated the status (optional, where applicable)

name: BlockMaterialLocationsRequest
description: Sent to a process endpoint to block or disable a particular material setup location. This is typically used where a loaded material may become unsuitable for use, for example MSD expiry of an SMT material.
properties: BlockMaterialLocationsRequest: Default constructor | Comments: Free form comments indicating additional information about this block event. | Locations: A list of locations to be blocked | Reason: An enumeration indicating the reason for the block.

name: BlockMaterialLocationsResponse
description: Sent to a process endpoint to block or disable a particular material setup location. This is typically used where a loaded material may become unsuitable for use, for example MSD expiry of an SMT material.
properties: BlockMaterialLocationsResponse: Default constructor | Result: The result of the request

name: GetActiveRecipeRequest
description: Used to request the name of the recipe that is activated at a process endpoint. The response indicates the name of the recipe.
properties: GetActiveRecipeRequest:  | Lane: The optional number of the production lane | Stage: The optional structure identifying the specific Stage

name: GetActiveRecipeResponse
description: Used to request the name of the recipe that is activated at a process endpoint. The response indicates the name of the recipe.
properties: GetActiveRecipeResponse: Default constructor | ActiveRecipeName: The name of the active recipe (may include full path, if applicable) | ActiveRecipeRevision: Version number, e.g. “2.0” (Optional) | Result: The result of the request

name: GetAvailableRecipesRequest
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** This message is used to request a process endpoint for the available recipes.
properties: GetAvailableRecipesRequest:  | MaxCount: Max. number of recipes that may be returned with the response. | Path: Pathname with wildcard, e.g. “myRecipes/CFX/A-Team*”.

name: GetAvailableRecipesResponse
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Response to a request of getting the available recipes. The response includes a list of recipes (name, revision), but not their data.
properties: GetAvailableRecipesResponse: Default Constructor | ActualCount: The actual number of recipes that match the request. | RecipeCount: The number of recipes in the response list | Recipes: An optional list of the identified RecipeLean (RecipeName and Revision) structures | Result: The result of the request

name: GetRecipeRequest
description: This message is used to request a process endpoint for the details of a named recipe. The response includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process.
properties: GetRecipeRequest:  | RecipeName: The name of the recipe to be retrieved (may include full path, if applicable) | Revision: Version number, e.g. “2.0” (Optional)

name: GetRecipeResponse
description: This message is used to request a process endpoint for the details of a named recipe. The response includes details of the recipe, depending on the classification of the process.
properties: GetRecipeResponse: Default Constructor | Recipe: The recipe. This is a dynamic structure tha supports specialized recipe types for different types of equipment. | Result: The result of the request

name: GetRequiredSetupRequest
description: Sent to a process endpoint to request the setup requirements of the active recipe. The response lists the required materials and tools, along with the locations where the materials/tools must be loaded.
properties: GetRequiredSetupRequest:  | Lane: An optional property designating the specific Lane. | Stage: An optional property designating the specific Stage.

name: GetRequiredSetupResponse
description: Response from a process endpoint to a request to obtain the setup requirements of the active recipe. The response lists the required materials and tools, along with the locations where the materials/tools must be loaded.
properties: GetRequiredSetupResponse: Default constructor | Lane: An optional property designating the specific Lane associated with this setup. | RecipeName: The name of the recipe associated with the required setup. | RecipeRevision: The revision of the recipe that is required (eg. "A", "B", etc.) | Result: Result of the request | SetupRequirements: A structure indicating the setup requirements. | Stage: An optional property designating the specific Stage associated with this setup.

name: GetUnitInfoRequest

If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. | UnitIdentifier: Unique ID of Production Unit, e.g. Barcode, to be used in case the Primary Identifier is not available and, thus, PrimaryIdentifier is left empty.

maximum amount of power recorded during the sample period (in watts) | PeakPowerRYB: The maximum amount of power recorded during the sample period on three phases (in watts) | PeakVoltage: The maximum amount of voltage recorded during the sample period (in Volts DC or AC (RMS)) | PeakVoltageRYB: The maximum amount of voltage recorded during the sample period on three phases (in Volts DC or AC (RMS)) | PowerFactorNow: Description (Coefficient number between 0.0 and 1.0 ) | PowerFactorNowRYB: Description (Coefficient number between 0.0 and 1.0 ) | PowerNow: The instantaneous amount of power recorded at EndTime (in watts) | PowerNowRYB: The instantaneous amount of power recorded at EndTime on three phases (in watts) | Result: The result of the request | StartTime: The start time of the sample period. Start time is set as the start time of the immediately prior CFX.ResourcePerformance.EnergyConsumed message | ThreePhaseNeutralCurrentNow: The instantanous amount of current on Neutral at EndTime (in Ampere) | VoltageNow: The instantaneous value of voltage recorded at EndTime (in Volts DC or AC (RMS)) | VoltageNowRYB: The instantaneous amount of voltage recorded at EndTime on three phases (in Volts DC or AC (RMS))

name: FaultAcknowledged
description: Sent when a fault has been acknowledged by the operator, but not yet corrected (cleared). A subsequent FaultCleared message will be sent once the operator addresses the issue.
properties: FaultAcknowledged: Default Constructor | FaultOccurrenceId: A unique identifier of the instance of the related fault. Corresponds with the FaultOccurrenceId property of the corresponding FaultOccurred message | Operator: The operator or entity who has acknowledged the fault (if known, otherwise null) (optional)

name: FaultCleared
description: Sent by a process endpoint when a fault is cleared as described in a FaultOccurred message
properties: FaultCleared: Default Constructor | FaultOccurrenceId: A unique identifier of the instance of the related fault. Corresponds with the FaultOccurrenceId property of the corresponding FaultOccurred message | Operator: The operator or entity who has cleared the fault (if known, otherwise null) (optional)

name: FaultOccurred
description: Sent by a process endpoint whenever a fault is encountered. A data structure must be included in the message related to specific equipment type.
properties: FaultOccurred: Default Constructor | Fault: Dynamic structure providing detailed information about the fault that has occurred.

name: GetActiveFaultsRequest
description: This request allows an external source to query the equipment for a list of active faults. message that it sends back to the requester.
properties: GetActiveFaultsRequest: Default Constructor

name: GetActiveFaultsResponse
description: Response to an external source to modify a generic configuration parameter of a process endpoint.
properties: GetActiveFaultsResponse: Initializes a new instance of the GetActiveFaultsResponse class. | ActiveFaults: A list of faults that are currently active at the endpoint | Result: The result of the request

name: HandleFaultRequest
description: This request allows an external source to modify the behaviour for this dedicated fault in such a way that the equoipment itself is not indicating the operator to handle this fault.
properties: HandleFaultRequest: Default Constructor | FaultOccurrenceId: A 128-bit unique identifier which uniquely identifier this specific occurrence of the fault | HandleRemote: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the fault should be handled remotely or not. If handled remotely, the red light should be off on the equipment (at least for this fault)

name: HandleFaultResponse
description: Response to an external source to modify the behaviour for the equipment to guide the operator process endpoint.
properties: HandleFaultResponse: Initializes a new instance of the HandleFaultResponse class. | Result: The result of the request

name: LogEntryRecorded
description: An informational message sent by a process endpoint regarding the something that has occurred at the station. Information about the criticality of the information should also be given (information, warning, error etc.).
properties: LogEntryRecorded: Default constructor | AdditionalData: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.4 ** Optional additional data depending on the machine type | Importance: The relative importance of the log entry | InformationId: An endpoint-specific string that defines the nature of the log entry | Lane: The production lane related to the log entry (where applicable) | Message: A human-friendly description of the log entry | Stage:

name: MaintenancePerformed
description: Sent by an endpoint when maintenance has been performed. Information includes the type of maintenance, maintenance code, parts used, labor etc.
properties: MaintenancePerformed: Default constructor | ConsumedMaterials: A list of the specific parts and materials that were consumed while performing the maintenance event | MaintenanceJobCode: An endpoint-specific code indicating the nature of the maintenance event that was conducted | MaintenanceOrderNumber: The work order number related to this maintenance event | MaintenanceType: An enumeration describing the type of maintenance that was performed | Tasks: A list of the specific tasks that were performed while conducting this maintenance event

name: ModifyStationParametersRequest
description: This request allows an external source to modify a generic configuration parameter of a process endpoint. Upon successful processing of this request, the endpoint should publish a message in addition to the message that it sends back to the requester.
properties: ModifyStationParametersRequest: Default Constructor | NewParameters: A list of the paramters to be modified. The Parameter structure is a dynamic structure, and may be of differing types depending on the type of endpoint.

name: ModifyStationParametersResponse
description: Response to an external source to modify a generic configuration parameter of a process endpoint.
properties: ModifyStationParametersResponse: Default constructor | Result: The result of the request

name: ProcessDataRecorded
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.
properties: ProcessDataRecorded:  | ProcessData: Process data recorded

name: SleepStateChanged
description: ** NOTE: ADDED in CFX 1.3 ** Sent by a process endpoint when the sleep state transitions from one state to another.
properties: SleepStateChanged: Default Constructor | Lane: The relevant production lane (if applicable) | NewSleepState: The new sleep state | OldSleepState: An enumeration indicating the previous sleep state prior to this state change | Stage: The stage that has changed sleep state. If omitted then the sleep state for the whole station has been changed. | TransitionTimeRemaining: If the new sleep state requires less wake up time than the old, this indicates that the state is in the process of being changed. Time interval can be nullable (for example if there is no time remaining).

name: StageStateChanged
description: Sent by a process endpoint when the production state of one of its stages transitions from one state to another per its state model.
properties: StageStateChanged: Default Constructor | Lane: The relevant production lane (if applicable) | NewE58State: Exposes vendor specific new state code (like 3312, for example), as its equivalent Semi E58 enumerated value. READ-ONLY HELPER PROPERTY, NOT A DATA PROPERTY. WILL NOT APPEAR IN JSON DATA. | NewState: The new state | OldE58State: Exposes vendor specific old state code (like 3312, for example) as its equivalent Semi E58 enumerated value. READ-ONLY HELPER PROPERTY, NOT A DATA PROPERTY. WILL NOT APPEAR IN JSON DATA. | OldState: The previous state prior to this state change | OldStateDuration: The amount of time spent in the previous state. Note: There may be certain circumstances where it is impossible to provide this duration (as in the case of an unexpected power faillure or other extreme events). In this case it is acceptable to report a duration of "null", which will be interpreted as "unknown" by the receiver of this event. | RelatedFault: In the case of a stoppage, information about the Fault which caused the stoppage. Otherwise null. | Stage: The name of the stage that has changed state

name: StationOffline
description: Sent by a process endpoint when it is about to transition to a state where it is not possible for further communication, for example when the endpoint is powered down, reset, put into maintenance mode, or simply set off-line.
properties: StationOffline:

name: StationOnline
description: Sent by a process endpoint when it is ready for communication to resume, for example, powered up, maintenance over, etc.
properties: StationOnline:  | OfflineDuration: The total amount of time the endpoint was offline prior to this notification. Note: There may be certain circumstances where it is impossible to provide this duration (as in the case of an unexpected power faillure or other extreme events). In this case it is acceptable to report a duration of "null", which will be interpreted as "unknown" by the receiver of this event.

name: StationParametersModified
description: Sent by a process endpoint to indicate that an operator has modified a generic parameter or configuration setting. This does not apply to settings related to recipes, which are handled by the RecipeModified event.
properties: StationParametersModified: Default Constructor | ModifiedParameters: A list of the paramters that have been modified, along with their new values. The Parameter structure is a dynamic structure, and may be of differing types depending on the type of endpoint.

name: IdentifiersNotRead
description: Sent by an identification device (barcode scanner, RFID reader, etc.) when the device attempts to read or interpret an identifier, but is unable to do so.
properties: IdentifiersNotRead: Default Constructor | PositionsNotRead: And optional property indicated the position that cannot be read. Used in the case that multiple production units are moving through the process, and the sensor is capable if reading multiple identifiers.

name: IdentifiersRead
description: Sent by an identification device (barcode scanner, RFID reader, etc.
properties: IdentifiersRead:  | HermesIdentifier: The Hermes BoardId of the carrier, a PCB panel or single production unit. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. HermesIdentifier will be transfered between all machines which support Hermes. The PrimaryIdentifier is containing a barcode information. Both can be transferred. Remarks Espcially when the line does not support the Hermes standard in the hole line, the Hermes Identifier can be unique only in the a part of the line. The Primary Identifier can be used to correlate the parts of hermes sublines to correlate this data as well. | PrimaryIdentifier: The barcode, RFID, etc. that was most recently acquired by the scanner / reader. If a single production unit is moving through the process, this would be the actual unique identifier of that individual unition unit. However, if multiple production units are moving through the process as a group (as in the case of a PCB panel, a fixture, or any sort of carrier), this would be an identifier that represents the entire group of units, such as a carrier UID, a PCB panel UID, etc. | Units: An optional list of actual production unit identifiers, in the case that multiple production units are moving through the process, and the sensor is capable if reading multiple identifiers.
